transcript.user: Search the radarr logs for why the last release got rejected.
replies:
Here's what I found for the most recent rejection:

**Queen & Slim (2019)** — release `Queen.and.Slim.2019.1080p.BluRay.DD+7.1.x264-iFT`

Radarr sent it to SABnzbd twice (18:28 and 18:31 on July 3), but SABnzbd aborted it both times with the reason: **"cannot be completed"** — the usenet post was missing articles/parts, so SAB couldn't assemble the full file. Radarr then blocklisted it so it won't try that same release again.

This isn't a Radarr config or permission issue — it's a dead/broken release on the indexer. If you want, I can un-blocklist it and have Radarr search for a different release of Queen & Slim instead.

replies:
Here's what I found:

**No active downloads right now** — the queue is empty, unpaused, no speed cap set. So there's nothing currently slow to measure directly.

**NAS is healthy:**
- CPU load: 0.2 (idle)
- Memory: 24% used, no swap pressure
- All services reachable in 7-12ms
- Downloads disk: 381 GB free of 468 GB — plenty of room

**Internet speed test** — I tried running one from the NAS but it didn't return actual numbers (sandbox limitation). I can't give you a live speed figure from here.

**Recent history:** The only alerts in the last few days were AdGuard DNS having two brief hiccups on July 3rd (resolved within a minute each) and the ailogueos node flapping on July 2nd — nothing that would explain sustained slow downloads.

**Bottom line:** There's nothing wrong right now — no queue, no load, no throttling. If downloads *were* slow recently, I'd need more to go on. A few things I can check if you want:

- SABnzbd's own history for recent download speeds
- Prowlarr indexer health (maybe a specific indexer was slow)
- Whether the ISP was throttling Usenet (that's outside my tools, but I can escalate it)
